key_insight: A Swin3D Transformer surrogate can learn electrode-scale battery discharge dynamics directly from 3D microstructure volumes, replacing expensive physics simulations with fast predictions.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid screening and optimization of lithium-ion electrode designs by giving engineers a scalable way to estimate spatiotemporal discharge behavior without running full electrochemical models.
method: Swin3D-based deep surrogate processes volumetric electrode data to predict discharge fields over time.
Gaussian Positional Encoding improves spatial learning for complex microstructure geometry.
Temporal Encoding captures nonlinear evolution across discharge timesteps.
evidence_signal: On an Electrochemical Simulation dataset, the method outperforms state-of-the-art point cloud baselines in prediction accuracy while reducing computational overhead by orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the representativeness and quality of the training simulations, so generalization to unseen electrode chemistries or morphologies should be validated carefully.
paper_title: AI-Driven Surrogate Models for Predicting Electrode-Scale Discharge Behavior in Lithium-Ion Batteries
